question: Are there exactly three transparent, sealed sample bottles visible on the workbench?
Is the scene clearly situated in a laboratory fume hood environment (e.g., visible sash, baffles, ventilation interior)?
Do the bottle labels textually display "Hg", "Br2", and "I2" respectively, without additional readable explanatory words?
Is a thermometer visible nearby that clearly reads "22°C"?
Does the bottle labeled "Hg" (Mercury) contain a substance that appears as a silvery, metallic liquid?
Does the bottle labeled "Br2" (Bromine) contain a deep reddish-brown liquid (potentially with visible colored vapor)?
Does the bottle labeled "I2" (Iodine) contain dark grey or purple-black solid crystals (not a liquid)?
Are laboratory safety elements (such as gloves or safety goggles) visible in the scene?
Are generic hazard pictograms present on the bottles or nearby, without readable text?
Is the image a close-up, realistic photograph (photorealistic textures, lighting, depth of field)?
Are there any explanatory annotations, captions, or callouts added to the image beyond the bottle labels and thermometer reading?
